name: Circuito do Beliche — circular route (PR2)
region_intro: Jogging around Azinhal offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, agricultural interior with carob and olive groves, and extensive cork oak and holm oak forests. The region features quiet roads and paths, providing a serene environment for runners away from heavy traffic. Trails often lead through the fertile Mira River Valley, offering views of the Monchique Range, and provide access to features like the Santa Clara Dam and the Guadiana River. This varied terrain ensures a range of jogging experiences, from gentle slopes to more challenging inclines. Best jogging routes around Azinhal The most popular jogging route is Odeleite from up close and from afar — circular route (PR4), a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This circular route offers varied terrain and scenic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Corte António Martins — circular route (PR1), a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This route winds through agricultural lands and offers glimpses of the region's natural beauty. Local runners also love the Circuito do Beliche — circular route (PR2), a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through quiet paths near the Beliche Dam, often completed in about 45 minutes. Jogging around Azinhal is defined by rolling hills, cork oak forests, and river valleys, with quiet roads and paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs. The routes in Azinhal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 6000 runners have used komoot to explore Azinhal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Azinhal?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 75 running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Azinhal, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy jogs to more challenging runs through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or families in Azinhal?
A: Yes, Azinhal offers several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or families. There are &lt;strong&gt;5 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; available, characterized by gentle slopes and quiet paths. These routes allow you to enjoy the natural beauty without excessive challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Azinhal?
A: Jogging trails in Azinhal feature a diverse landscape, including rolling hills, agricultural areas with carob, olive, and almond groves, and extensive cork oak and holm oak forests. You'll also find paths along the Mira River Valley and near impressive water bodies like the Beliche Dam. The terrain varies from gentle slopes to more challenging inclines, ensuring a varied running experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Azinhal?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Azinhal are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418000441/odeleite-from-up-close-and-from-afar-circular-route-pr4" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odeleite from up close and from afar — circular route (PR4)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421632058/corte-antonio-martins-circular-route-pr1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corte António Martins — circular route (PR1)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1417972615/circuito-do-beliche-circular-route-pr2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuito do Beliche — circular route (PR2)&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you near the Beliche Dam.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can see while running in Azinhal?
A: Azinhal's running routes offer numerous scenic views. You can enjoy vistas of the Monchique Range, the Mira River Valley, and the Guadiana River. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2957551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odeleite Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5683369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beliche Dam&lt;/a&gt;, as well as historic windmills. The region's natural beauty, with its cork oak forests and agricultural fields, provides a picturesque backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Azinhal offers &lt;strong&gt;15 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. These trails typically feature greater elevation changes and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32241099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beliche Dam – View of the Odeleite Dam loop from Azinhal&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 17 km long and includes significant ascents.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Azinhal?
A: The running routes in Azinhal are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.1 stars from over 60 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Runners often praise the quiet roads and paths, the diverse natural landscapes, and the serene environment away from heavy traffic, making it an ideal spot for outdoor activity.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by lakes or dams in Azinhal?
A: Yes, several routes in Azinhal offer views of or pass near significant water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1417972615/circuito-do-beliche-circular-route-pr2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circuito do Beliche — circular route (PR2)&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through quiet paths near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5683369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beliche Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The impressive Santa Clara Dam, one of Europe's largest, is also nearby and offers expansive 'water mirror' views surrounded by forests.

Q: Is Azinhal a good place for long-distance running?
A: With &lt;strong&gt;79 routes&lt;/strong&gt; ranging in difficulty and length, Azinhal provides excellent opportunities for long-distance running. The varied terrain, including rolling hills and extensive forests, combined with quiet paths, makes it suitable for extended runs. The longest route listed is over 17 km, offering a substantial challenge.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the running trails?
A: While primarily known for its natural beauty, the region around Azinhal does have historical points of interest. You can find several castles nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/681925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Marim Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forte de São Sebastião&lt;/a&gt;, which could be explored after your run. The Azinhal Fountain, a 19th-century landmark, is also a pleasant spot.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Azinhal?
A: Azinhal's mild climate makes it suitable for jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes. Even in summer, the cork oak and holm oak forests provide shaded paths, though it's advisable to run early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the midday heat.

Q: Are the running routes in Azinhal well-marked?
A: Many of the routes in Azinhal are part of established hiking and cycling networks, such as the PR (Pequena Rota) trails, which are generally well-marked. This ensures a clear path for runners, allowing you to focus on your run and the beautiful surroundings.
tips: From this point on the road you have a privileged panorama of Albufeira de Beliche. It is the first moment that the dam emerges after leaving the parking lot.

If you visit this reservoir, be sure to visit the other dam, Odeleite, a few kilometers further north, which is almost even more beautiful. At both dams you will find hiking trails, peace and quiet, and sleepy villages.
Near Beliche, on the circular route of the Dam, the dirt road passes under the road viaduct. A very beautiful place, and one of the only shady stretches along the way.
